title: Simulation: Mini Startup
perfectScoreMsg: You got {{score}} out of {{total}} scenarios correct! You understand mini startup economics!
lessonLabel: Lesson: Mini startups can be smart investments when you have good profit margins (40%+). Always calculate profit = Revenue - Cost!
lowScoreMsg: You got {{score}} out of {{total}} scenarios correct. Remember, profit = Revenue - Cost, and 40%+ profit is good!
tipLabel: Tip: Calculate profit = Revenue - Cost. If you invest ₹300 and earn ₹500, that's ₹200 profit (67% return) - that's smart!

scenarios.title: Mini Startup: Online Snack Delivery
scenarios.description: You have ₹500. Which strategy maximizes profit safely?

options.text: Invest entire ₹500 in ingredients without market check
Borrow extra ₹500 to buy more stock
Wait and save more before starting
Test small batch, learn demand, then expand

scenarios.title: Mini Startup: Handmade Crafts
scenarios.description: You have ₹400. What's the smart approach?

options.text: Spend all ₹400 on materials without profit estimate
Produce large quantity hoping to sell quickly
Estimate cost vs selling price, start small batch
Wait for trend to become popular

scenarios.title: Mini Startup: Lemonade Stand
scenarios.description: You have ₹300. What's the most strategic move?

options.text: Set up anywhere without checking foot traffic
Choose high foot-traffic area, budget ingredients wisely
Borrow ₹500 more to expand immediately
Save ₹300 instead of starting

scenarios.title: Mini Startup: Book Resale
scenarios.description: You have ₹600. How do you minimize risk?

options.text: Buy all books from seller without quality check
Borrow extra ₹400 to stock quickly
Wait for better deal later
Inspect sample books, estimate demand, buy accordingly

scenarios.title: Mini Startup: Tutoring Service
scenarios.description: You have ₹500. How do you maximize success?

options.text: Create a skill-focused plan, target audience, advertise smartly
Spend all on random ads without targeting
Hire someone without checking skills
Save money instead of starting business
